entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop being evaluated for resistance to seed infection by fungi.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A fungus that causes seed infection and aflatoxin contamination in peanuts.

entities.name: IN VITRO SEED COLONIZATION
entities.description: A laboratory-based evaluation method (IVSCAF) to test seed colonization resistance.

entities.name: FIELD RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to resist seed infection under field conditions.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in peanut seeds resulting from fungal infection.

entities.name: SOUTHERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the field trials were conducted.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS NIGER
entities.description: A soil fungus that causes seed infection in peanuts.

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: A soil fungus that causes seed infection in peanuts.

entities.name: FUSARIUM
entities.description: A genus of soil fungi that causes seed infection in peanuts.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN B1
entities.description: A specific type of aflatoxin associated with seed infection.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity level of the peanut genotypes.

entities.name: POD AND SEED CHARACTERS
entities.description: Physical attributes of the peanut pods and seeds.

entities.name: SEED INFECTION
entities.description: The process of seeds being colonized and infected by fungi.
